questions.q: When a partner is admitted into a firm, the profit and loss of the firm is shared among all the partners in their capital ratio?
questions.explanation: When a partner is admitted into a firm, the profit and loss of the firm is shared among all the partners in their capital ratio. So the statement is true.

questions.q: A new partner is admitted into a partnership firm with 1/4th share in the profits, the share of existing partners will be?
questions.explanation: If a new partner is admitted into a partnership firm with 1/4th share in the profits, the share of existing partners will be 3/4.

questions.q: The amount brought in by the new partner at the time of admission is called?
questions.explanation: The amount brought in by the new partner at the time of admission is called additional capital.

questions.q: A partner is admitted with a premium of Rs. 50,000. The amount is credited to the capital account of the existing partner?
questions.explanation: A partner is admitted with a premium of Rs. 50,000. The amount is credited to the capital account of the existing partner.

questions.q: The amount received from the existing partner at the time of admission of a new partner is called?
questions.explanation: The amount received from the existing partner at the time of admission of a new partner is called premium.

questions.q: A partner is admitted with a share of 1/3rd. The new partner brings in an additional capital of Rs. 1,50,000. The amount will be credited to?
questions.explanation: The amount will be credited to partner's current account.

questions.q: The ratio in which the goodwill of the firm is divided among the partners at the time of admission of a new partner?
questions.explanation: The ratio in which the goodwill of the firm is divided among the partners at the time of admission of a new partner is capital & profit ratio.

questions.q: A new partner is admitted into a firm with a share of 1/4th. The goodwill of the firm is valued at Rs. 2,00,000. The amount will be credited to?
questions.explanation: The amount will be credited to old partner's capital account.

questions.q: The value of goodwill at the time of admission of a new partner is equal to the?
opts: (A) Average profits of the firm for the last 3 years
(B) Average profits of the firm for the last 5 years
(B) Average profits of the firm for the last 3 years & 5 years
questions.explanation: The value of goodwill at the time of admission of a new partner is equal to the average profits of the firm for the last 3 years & 5 years.

questions.q: A partner is admitted with a share of 1/3rd. The goodwill of the firm is valued at Rs. 2,50,000. The amount will be credited to?
questions.explanation: The amount will be credited to partner's current account.

questions.q: The goodwill of the firm is valued at Rs. 3,00,000. The amount is credited to the new partner's capital account?
questions.explanation: The goodwill of the firm is valued at Rs. 3,00,000. The amount is credited to the new partner's capital account.

questions.q: A partner is admitted with a share of 1/2nd. The goodwill of the firm is valued at Rs. 4,00,000. The amount is credited to the new partner's capital account?
questions.explanation: The amount is credited to the new partner's capital account.

questions.explanation: The goodwill of the firm is valued at Rs. 5,00,000. The amount is credited to the new partner's capital account.
